comment: The tool-neutral integration contract for nervepack. The onboarding agent satisfies each capability for its host and records HOW (+ a verify command) in its adapter.json. np-doctor.sh reads this file: 'check:core' capabilities are verified by the doctor's shipped host-neutral checks; 'check:adapter' capabilities are verified by running the command the adapter recorded. MUST failures are errors; SHOULD failures are warnings; a host that cannot satisfy a SHOULD marks it 'unsupported'.

capabilities.title: nervepack skills are reachable by the model every session
capabilities.why: The whole point: the model must see skills/ (and the consult-nervepack directive) or nervepack does nothing.
capabilities.accept: Starting a fresh session, the model can name/use a known skill without being handed the file by the user.

hints.goose: expose skills via .goosehints (point at INDEX.md) and/or a recipe that loads the skill dir.
hints.generic: make the markdown under skills/ part of the model's standing/system context each session.
hints.cursor: Cursor reads AGENTS.md + .cursor/rules/*.mdc natively (skills as standing context). For the consult-nervepack directive on a host without a session-start hook, append the managed block: setup/np-instruction-block.sh install <project>/AGENTS.md (or .cursor/rules/nervepack.mdc).
hints.codex: Codex reads AGENTS.md natively. Add the directive via the managed block: setup/np-instruction-block.sh install AGENTS.md.
hints.instruction-file: For any host that surfaces standing context through an instruction file but has NO session-start hook, run setup/np-instruction-block.sh install <native instruction file>. It appends only a fenced @import block (additive, idempotent, removable) — never run it on a host that already injects the directive via a session-start hook (e.g. Claude Code), to avoid double-injection. Verify: grep -q 'nervepack:begin' <file>.

capabilities.title: np-llm.sh returns text for a stdin prompt
capabilities.why: Capture, evaluator and maintenance all summarize/judge/act through this one wrapper; if it can't reach a model, the runtime is dead.
capabilities.accept: echo a prompt | np-llm.sh complete -> non-empty text on stdout.

hints.claude-code: default backend; NP_LLM_BACKEND=claude, CLAUDE_BIN points at the claude CLI.
hints.goose: set NP_LLM_BACKEND=goose (or ollama) and the model env; np-llm.sh routes complete/agent to it.
hints.generic: set NP_LLM_BACKEND + NP_LLM_MODEL_CHEAP/_AGENT so np-llm.sh can shell out to your model. For a local model set NP_LLM_BACKEND=local + NP_LLM_BASE_URL (OpenAI-compatible) + NP_LLM_MODEL_CHEAP; agent mode needs NP_LLM_AGENT_CMD.

capabilities.title: the repo is a clone of origin and can fast-forward sync
capabilities.why: nervepack is git-synced across machines; knowledge and committed layers flow through origin/main.
capabilities.accept: git rev-parse inside ~/Code/nervepack succeeds and an origin remote is configured.

hints.generic: clone https://github.com/pat-browne/nervepack to ~/Code/nervepack; gh auth login for push.

capabilities.title: feature toggles resolve
capabilities.why: Every runtime behavior is gated through np_enabled; a broken toggle layer silently disables features.

hints.generic: ship setup/toggles.conf as-is; per-machine overrides in ~/.config/nervepack/toggles.local.

capabilities.title: the engine can locate a content overlay (skills + memory dirs)
capabilities.why: Engine machinery is reusable; a user's skills/episodic/playbooks/strategies/sources/wiki live in a content overlay resolved by np_content_dir. Default is the repo root (single-repo install).
capabilities.accept: np_content_dir resolves to an existing directory.

hints.generic: set NP_CONTENT_DIR or ~/.config/nervepack/content-dir to your content repo; default is the engine repo root.

capabilities.title: the optional team content overlay is configured and the toggle is on
capabilities.why: A team overlay merges shared skills/rules on top of the personal content layer; optional — unconfigured installs skip it entirely.
capabilities.accept: np_team_dir resolves (or is unconfigured — both are PASS).

hints.generic: set NP_TEAM_DIR or ~/.config/nervepack/team-dir to a shared content repo; enable the 'team' toggle to merge it.

capabilities.title: the dashboard/data→content symlink bridge resolves
capabilities.why: dashboard/index.html loads data/metrics.js as a relative sibling; in a split layout that file lives in the content overlay, so the engine's dashboard/data must be a symlink into that overlay. Without it a fresh clone shows no metrics.
capabilities.accept: In a split layout, <engine>/dashboard/data is a symlink that resolves to an existing directory. In a single-repo layout, dashboard/data is a real directory. Either way, the dashboard can read its data.

hints.generic: run engine/setup/35-link-dashboard-data.sh — it creates or repairs the symlink idempotently. Set NP_CONTENT_DIR or ~/.config/nervepack/content-dir first so the script knows where the content overlay lives.

capabilities.title: sync + consult-nervepack directive fire at session start
capabilities.why: Keeps the repo current and forces sessions to actually consult nervepack instead of working from scratch.
capabilities.accept: A new session triggers 40-sync and injects nervepack-session-directive.

hints.claude-code: SessionStart hooks in ~/.claude/settings.json (reference: 50/51). Also wire per-prompt recall hooks (UserPromptSubmit -> episodic-recall, playbook-recall, strategy-recall; reference: 52/53) so past notes/playbooks/strategies are injected on topic match.
hints.claude-code-windows: Same SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit hooks, but Claude Code on Windows runs hook commands via PowerShell by default — so the install scripts route each .sh command through Git-bash. np-hook-lib.sh does this automatically on a MINGW/MSYS kernel (or force with NP_HOOK_WRAP=1): the stored command becomes `bash -lc '<original>'`. Requires Git for Windows. Adapter: onboard/adapters/claude-code-windows.example.json.
hints.goose: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hooks in ~/.agents/plugins/nervepack/hooks/hooks.json (Open Plugins spec); map the recall hooks to your prompt-submit event.
hints.generic: wire your host's session-start event to run 40-sync-nervepack.sh + emit the directive, and its per-prompt event to run the *-recall.sh hooks.

capabilities.title: the session is captured to the episodic inbox on exit
capabilities.why: Immediate working-memory capture so the next session can recall where you left off (cron is only a backup).
capabilities.accept: Ending a session runs episodic-capture.sh against the transcript and writes an inbox note.

hints.claude-code: SessionEnd hook -> episodic-capture.sh session-end (reference: 52 install script).
hints.goose: SessionEnd hook (Open Plugins spec) -> episodic-capture.sh, mapping the host payload to {transcript_path,cwd,session_id}.
hints.generic: if your host has no session-end event, wrap it: run the host, then run episodic-capture after it exits.

capabilities.title: evaluator + promotion run on exit
capabilities.why: Scores the session and promotes inbox -> episodic themes + metrics immediately; crons become backup.
capabilities.accept: Ending a session runs np-evaluator.sh and np-session-flush.sh.

hints.claude-code: SessionEnd hooks -> np-evaluator.sh and np-session-flush.sh (reference: 54/55 install scripts).
hints.generic: same trigger as capture; the flush carries the NERVEPACK_AGENT guard so it won't recurse. Set NP_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/NP_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL for commit attribution on headless/cloud runs.

capabilities.title: promote / aggregate / skill-maintain / refine / compact run on a schedule
capabilities.why: The backup cadence that consolidates memory, metrics and skill budgets even if on-exit promotion is missed; refine/compact keep skills lint-clean and deduped.
capabilities.accept: A scheduler (cron or host-native) runs 71/72/73/75/76/77 on their cadences.

hints.claude-code: user crontab (reference: 70-install-memory-cron.sh). 76 (refine, Sunday) and 77 (compact, Wednesday) are default-on via maintain.refine / maintain.compact toggles.
hints.claude-code-macos: launchd LaunchAgents, not crontab (reference: 70-install-memory-launchd.sh); verify with `launchctl list | grep com.nervepack`. Adapter: onboard/adapters/claude-code-macos.example.json.
hints.claude-code-windows: Windows Task Scheduler, not crontab (reference: 70-install-memory-schtasks.sh; runs under Git-bash, shells to schtasks.exe); verify with `schtasks //Query //FO LIST | grep -i nervepack`. Requires Git for Windows. Adapter: onboard/adapters/claude-code-windows.example.json.
hints.generic: any scheduler that can run the 7x scripts; they self-gate on toggles and are idempotent. Set NP_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/NP_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL for commit attribution on headless/cloud runs. Optional offload: 76/77 can also run as cloud routines or OSS runners instead of local crons — see https://github.com/pat-browne/nervepack/issues/16 for the planned NP_SCHED_BACKEND seam.
